metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare that the ACLI credential used for teardown holds only project-admin (not global admin) rights scoped to the isolated test project, and document this as part of the isolation approach required by AC 'Isolation approach chosen and documented'.
findings.finding: The AC requires 'test issues hard-deleted in Jira', which demands delete-issue admin permission. The plan does not specify that the test credential's permission scope is restricted to the isolated test project, creating a risk that an admin-scoped credential could delete issues in production projects if test/production project isolation is misconfigured.
findings.location: AC: 'ZERO surviving test artifacts after a run: test issues hard-deleted in Jira' / Scope & isolation section
findings.suggested_fix: Create a dedicated Jira service account with 'Delete Issues' permission granted only on the test project (via project role, not global permission). Document the permission set alongside the isolation approach. Optionally add a pre-delete assertion that confirms the issue key belongs to the expected test project before executing the delete.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the observable assertion predicate for each named signal (C1 conflict signal, C2 create_after_hard_delete, C3 field-allowlist-drop alert, C4 429/Retry-After) inline in the What-matrix or in a linked glossary so an executing agent knows exactly what log output, return value, or state to assert against.
findings.finding: The What-matrix references C1, C2, C3, and C4 as named test-assertion targets ('emits the C1 signal', 'Hard-delete -> re-create (C2)', 'field-allowlist-drop alert (C3)', 'bulk-path 429/Retry-After (C4, fault-injected if needed)'), but no signal vocabulary or assertion predicate is defined anywhere in the plan for any of them. An executing agent cannot determine what observable system output constitutes a PASS for each signal.
findings.location: What section — matrix bullets: 'Deletion/tombstone... Hard-delete -> re-create (C2); field-allowlist-drop alert (C3); bulk-path 429/Retry-After (C4, fault-injected if needed)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a table or inline notes in the What section, e.g.: 'C1 = inbound diff produces a decision with reason="conflict" in the inbound_differ output; C2 = apply_inbound emits an action="create_after_hard_delete" mutation; C3 = update_one logs a stripped-fields warning to stderr listing the dropped key(s); C4 = acli_subprocess retries after a 429 and the final result is successful (or budget exhausted with structured log).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the new harness uses a `trap CLEANUP EXIT` (bash) or `pytest` fixture/`try/finally` (Python) so teardown runs unconditionally even on `set -e` exit or assertion failure — not inline after the test body.
findings.finding: The plan's AC-1 requires 'GUARANTEED bilateral teardown even on failure', but the only existing teardown vehicle (`e2e_validation_probe.sh` Phase 6) runs inline after `set -euo pipefail` — a mid-run failure causes Phase 6 to be skipped entirely, defeating the guarantee. The new harness must use a `trap ... EXIT` or Python `finally`/`pytest` fixture, neither of which is mentioned in the plan.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC-1; scripts/jira-pressure-test/e2e_validation_probe.sh lines 450-487
findings.suggested_fix: For bash: add `trap cleanup EXIT` at the top and move Phase 6 into a `cleanup()` function. For Python pytest: use an `autouse` fixture with `yield` + `finally` that hard-deletes Jira issue and archives local ticket regardless of test outcome.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand the AC to include one observable pass criterion per matrix scenario (C1–C4, idempotency mutation_count==0, blast-radius trip threshold), so each can be independently verified rather than bundled under 'results recorded.'
findings.finding: Several matrix scenarios — C1 (conflict signal), C2 (hard-delete/re-create), C3 (field-allowlist-drop alert), C4 (bulk-path 429/Retry-After), blast-radius breaker trip, and the idempotency pass (mutation_count=0) — are specified in 'What' but have no corresponding per-scenario observable pass criterion in the AC. The AC only says 'results recorded on the ticket,' which is not an end-state-observable proof that each scenario passed or failed.
findings.location: What section (matrix bullets); Acceptance Criteria section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a teardown fallback protocol specifying what the harness does when cleanup itself fails (e.g., emit a named alert/manifest of undeleted artifacts, require manual escalation steps, or define a separate 'cleanup-only' re-run mode) so the 'GUARANTEED' claim is operationally grounded.
findings.finding: The GUARANTEED bilateral teardown AC specifies cleanup runs in a 'finally/fixture even on failure' but provides no fallback protocol for when teardown itself fails (e.g., Jira hard-delete returns 403/404, ACLI unavailable, partial cleanup leaves some artifacts). The plan's own Scope section acknowledges that local deletion is irreversible (append-only git events), so a failed local cleanup has no recovery path. No fallback or escalation path is defined.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Live-validation harness with GUARANTEED bilateral teardown (cleanup in a finally/fixture even on failure)' and 'ZERO surviving test artifacts after a run'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the minimum test-project binding-population size (or an explicit override of `max_acting_fraction`) needed to keep the blast-radius breaker below its threshold on 'normal pass' scenarios, so the 'no trip on normal pass; trips on synthetic mass-change' AC is testable without false positives.
findings.finding: The blast-radius breaker scenario ('no trip on a normal pass; trips on a synthetic mass-change') is mechanically unsound in an isolated test project with a small synthetic binding population: classify.check_blast_radius divides acting/total_bindings and the documented default cap is 0.10. With, e.g., 5 test bindings and 1 acting decision, acting_fraction=0.20 exceeds the cap — meaning the breaker trips on every 'normal' pass, making the two scenarios indistinguishable without a minimum population specification.
findings.location: What section: 'Blast-radius breaker: no trip on a normal pass; trips on a synthetic mass-change'
findings.suggested_fix: Either create enough synthetic bindings in the test project that a single normal mutation stays under 0.10 (i.e., ≥11 bindings so 1 acting → fraction=0.09), or specify that the harness overrides `REBAR_MAX_ACTING_FRACTION` for the normal-pass scenario and restores the production cap for the mass-change scenario.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace C1/C2/C3/C4 shorthand with explicit codebase-grounded descriptions: state the observable event type (e.g., specific BRIDGE_ALERT kind, quarantine mutation, or health-record field) each scenario asserts against.
findings.finding: The plan uses C1/C2/C3/C4 shorthand codes throughout the matrix without defining them in the plan body. C1 ('conflict signal'), C2 ('hard-delete → re-create'), C3 ('field-allowlist-drop alert'), and C4 ('bulk-path 429/Retry-After') are not named symbols in the codebase. A test harness author cannot resolve what observable codebase event each code maps to without out-of-band knowledge.
findings.location: What section — 'Hard-delete -> re-create (C2); field-allowlist-drop alert (C3); bulk-path 429/Retry-After (C4, fault-injected if needed)' and 'Blast-radius breaker … trips on a synthetic mass-change'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a legend: C1 = conflict quarantine mutation emitted by differ; C2 = BRIDGE_ALERT 'create-identity-write-failed' after hard-delete+recreate; C3 = [define or remove]; C4 = Retry-After header honored in acli_subprocess.py 429 retry path.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how the harness handles binding-store commits on the tickets branch during teardown: either (a) the harness provisions a fully isolated tickets branch (throwaway worktree) so teardown can drop the branch entirely, or (b) the teardown explicitly removes test-ticket binding entries from bindings.json and commits that removal to the tickets branch.
findings.finding: The plan's bilateral teardown goal ('ZERO surviving test artifacts … test tickets archived/deleted locally') does not address that the binding store's _commit_binding_store_snapshot writes binding entries to the tickets orphan branch as git commits. A teardown that only archives test tickets locally and hard-deletes Jira issues leaves orphaned binding-store commits on the tickets branch, which is not 'ZERO surviving test artifacts' in the local system.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria: 'ZERO surviving test artifacts after a run: test issues hard-deleted in Jira; test tickets archived/deleted locally; a leaked-artifact sweep runs before AND after'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reframe this AC as an observable outcome: e.g., 'The harness exits 0 with all matrix scenarios green, and a structured test-report artifact (JSON or JUnit XML) is attached to the ticket showing each scenario as PASS.'
findings.finding: AC 'Comprehensive matrix above executed against live Jira; results recorded on the ticket' is effort-framed ('executed … recorded') rather than outcome-observable; it does not state what a passing result looks like or how to distinguish pass from fail.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Comprehensive matrix above executed against live Jira; results recorded on the ticket'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a teardown-failure fallback spec: teardown must retry cleanup calls with backoff, and on persistent failure must log/emit all un-deleted artifact IDs (Jira issue keys + local ticket IDs) to a durable location so manual remediation is possible — ensuring the 'GUARANTEED' claim holds under Jira API degradation.
findings.finding: The teardown guarantee is asserted but does not specify how teardown-phase failures (e.g., Jira 429/5xx during hard-delete, network timeout) are handled — if a cleanup API call fails, there is no described fallback (log artifact IDs for manual remediation, retry with backoff) to honor the 'GUARANTEED' claim.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1 ('GUARANTEED bilateral teardown') and AC3 ('ZERO surviving test artifacts')
findings.suggested_fix: In the harness design, wrap each cleanup call in retry-with-backoff (e.g., 3 attempts, exponential). On exhaustion, append the artifact ID to a 'leaked-artifacts.log' file and surface a non-zero exit code. The pre-run sweep AC already implies reading this log — make it explicit.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a preflight capability check that confirms the test Jira account has issue-delete permission in the target project (e.g., probe `delete_issue` on a canary issue created for that purpose, or assert `DELETE /rest/api/3/mypermissions` returns `DELETE_ISSUES: true`), and document the fallback (transition to Done + label) if delete permission is unavailable.
findings.finding: The AC asserts 'test issues hard-deleted in Jira' and 'ZERO surviving artifacts' but the codebase's delete_issue implementation explicitly handles 403/PermissionError as a soft-fail (acli.py:716-720), meaning teardown silently leaves artifacts if the test account lacks delete permission — a common Jira project restriction. This capability is unverified for the target test project.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'ZERO surviving test artifacts after a run: test issues hard-deleted in Jira'
findings.suggested_fix: In the harness's setup phase, call `client.delete_issue(canary_key)` on a freshly-created probe issue and assert it returns `{"status": "deleted"}` not `{"status": "not_found"}` and does not raise PermissionError — fail fast with a clear message if delete is unavailable, rather than silently degrading teardown.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define C1–C4 signals inline in the plan (or a linked spec section): state the exact observable artifact each signal produces (e.g., log line pattern, entity-property value, label name) so the harness can assert against a concrete target.
findings.finding: The C1, C2, C3, C4 signal labels are used in the matrix bullets without definition in the plan; an executor cannot write assertions for 'emits the C1 signal' or 'hard-delete -> re-create (C2)' without knowing their precise observable manifestations (e.g., which field/log/event constitutes each signal).
findings.location: What — matrix bullets (three-way-merge, hard-delete, field-allowlist, bulk-path items)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Signal definitions' subsection: e.g., 'C1: conflict event written to reconciler log with key conflict_signal and both local_ts and remote_ts; C2: new entity created with rebar-id matching original after hard-delete; C3: WARNING log line containing field_allowlist_drop; C4: reconciler respects Retry-After header, pauses ≥ stated seconds, then retries.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'fault-injected if needed' with a stated default mechanism (e.g., 'monkey-patch the HTTP client to return a 429 with Retry-After: 2 on the first bulk call') so the executor can implement it without a design decision.
findings.finding: 'fault-injected if needed' for the C4 (429/Retry-After) scenario defers the decision of whether and how to fault-inject to the executor, making it a placeholder rather than a decision.
findings.location: What — matrix bullet: 'bulk-path 429/Retry-After (C4, fault-injected if needed)'
findings.suggested_fix: State: 'C4: inject a synthetic 429 by monkey-patching the Jira HTTP adapter to return status=429 + Retry-After: 2 on the first bulk-update call; assert the reconciler pauses ≥2 s and retries successfully.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a proving step: run the harness with a deliberately injected mid-test exception and then execute the artifact-sweep query to confirm ZERO artifacts remain, making the teardown guarantee falsifiable.
findings.finding: No proving command exists for the 'GUARANTEED bilateral teardown' AC; the plan does not describe how to prove that teardown runs even on assertion failure/exception (e.g., a test that intentionally raises mid-run and then checks both systems for artifacts).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Live-validation harness with GUARANTEED bilateral teardown (cleanup in a finally/fixture even on failure)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a matrix entry: 'Teardown-under-failure: inject a RuntimeError after the first create step; assert both the Jira issue is hard-deleted and the local ticket is archived by running the artifact sweep immediately after.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'echo-marked comments' with precise mechanism language: specify that echo suppression is verified by confirming that a comment written outbound seeds the baseline so the next inbound diff emits zero mutations for that comment — not by checking for a per-comment mark field.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'echo-marked comments' are not re-ingested inbound, implying comments carry a literal echo-mark. The actual codebase mechanism is ProvenanceLedger baseline-seeding (differ.py:146, conflict_resolver.py:210-227): outbound writes seed the baseline so the subsequent inbound diff sees no change, rather than marking individual comments. No 'echo_mark' or 'echo_marked' symbol exists. A harness author targeting 'echo-marked comments' may instrument the wrong layer.
findings.location: What section — 'Echo suppression: our own writes (echo-marked comments, status, fields) are NOT re-ingested inbound'
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'echo-marked comments, status, fields' to 'comments/status/fields written outbound (baseline-seeded via ProvenanceLedger) are NOT re-ingested inbound — verify by confirming mutation_count=0 on a no-op second pass after an outbound write'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile AC item 3 ('ZERO surviving test artifacts … test tickets archived/deleted locally') with the Scope caveat: either (a) make the throwaway-env a hard requirement and restate AC 3 as 'ZERO surviving test artifacts in the throwaway env (git-history events in a discarded branch are acceptable),' or (b) explicitly add a back-out/discard step (e.g., branch deletion) as the rollback path for local irreversibility.
findings.finding: The Acceptance Criteria state 'ZERO surviving test artifacts after a run: test tickets archived/deleted locally' but the Scope & isolation section explicitly acknowledges that local 'deletion' is only a status change and underlying append-only events persist in git history. An implementer following the AC literally cannot satisfy 'ZERO surviving test artifacts' for the local side, while the Scope section only says to 'prefer' a throwaway env 'where possible' — sending the implementer in two directions about what 'done' means.
findings.location: AC item 3 vs. Scope & isolation section ('HONEST caveat')
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC item 3 to read: 'ZERO surviving test artifacts in the designated throwaway environment; local test tickets archived in the throwaway branch, which is deleted/discarded post-run (git-history events in the discarded branch are acceptable and expected). Runs against a non-throwaway env are explicitly out-of-scope.' Update the Scope section to make the throwaway env a hard requirement, not a preference.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare the credential injection mechanism for ACLI access (e.g., environment variable populated from a secrets manager, never hardcoded or CLI-flag), and specify that test-run output is scrubbed of secrets before being recorded on the ticket.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a Jira credential (API token or username/password for ACLI) on the boundary-crossing path but specifies nothing about how that credential is stored, injected, or prevented from appearing in recorded test output, logs, or the ticket comments where 'results are recorded'.
findings.location: AC: 'Comprehensive matrix above executed against live Jira; results recorded on the ticket' / Why section: 'An agent with ACLI access must validate'
findings.suggested_fix: Inject the Jira API token via a CI/CD secret or a local secrets manager (e.g., `export JIRA_API_TOKEN=$(vault read -field=token secret/jira)`) and configure ACLI to read it from the environment rather than a flag. Add a log-scrubbing step that masks the token pattern before output is written to the ticket.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Tighten this AC to name the specific observable: e.g., 'A second run produces the same test-report artifact (all scenarios PASS) and the artifact sweep returns zero on both Jira and local store, confirmed by running the sweep command after each run.'
findings.finding: 'Re-runnable: a second run leaves both systems in the same clean state (idempotent teardown)' — 'same clean state' is not a specific observable outcome; no command or assertion is given to verify both systems are in identical pre-run state after the second run.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Re-runnable: a second run leaves both systems in the same clean state (idempotent teardown)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the 429/Retry-After test mechanism before implementation: either (a) confirm a unit/integration test already covers this path (tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_error_taxonomy.py covers _call_with_backoff) and remove it from the live matrix, or (b) specify a concrete fault-injection approach (e.g., a thin HTTP proxy that injects 429 responses) compatible with the live-Jira harness.
findings.finding: The plan's matrix bullet 'bulk-path 429/Retry-After (C4, fault-injected if needed)' asserts the harness can exercise 429 rate-limit handling against live Jira, but this is an unverified capability: real Jira 429s are not reliably inducible on a test project without hitting production rate limits, and the plan offers no concrete mechanism for fault injection at the live-Jira boundary.
findings.location: What — matrix bullet: 'bulk-path 429/Retry-After (C4, fault-injected if needed)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify which transport path C4 targets (REST via urllib vs. ACLI subprocess), note that Retry-After is only observable on the REST path, and scope the fault-injection accordingly (e.g., mock urllib.error.HTTPError with a Retry-After header against the REST-path methods).
findings.finding: The C4 scenario (bulk-path 429/Retry-After) is partially ambiguous: the ACLI subprocess transport path raises subprocess.CalledProcessError with no HTTP header access, so Retry-After cannot be parsed there; only the REST transport path (acli_rest.py/_call_with_backoff via urllib.error.HTTPError) can read the header. A live harness must know which path it is exercising to assert C4 correctly.
findings.location: ## What — 'Hard-delete -> re-create (C2); field-allowlist-drop alert (C3); bulk-path 429/Retry-After (C4, fault-injected if needed)'
findings.suggested_fix: Amend the C4 bullet: 'bulk-path 429/Retry-After (C4) — inject via urllib.error.HTTPError against the REST transport path (_call_with_backoff in acli_subprocess.py); note the ACLI subprocess path (CalledProcessError) does not expose HTTP headers and is out of scope for Retry-After assertion.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit isolation gate: the harness must either (a) pause/fence the live reconciler for the test project scope during execution, or (b) document that the throwaway env runs no background reconciler, so test artifacts cannot be concurrently processed by a live sync cycle.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify that the live background reconciler (or any sync daemon already running against the same Jira project) must be paused, scoped, or fenced during harness execution. Test artifacts created in Jira could be ingested by the reconciler mid-run, producing real local state mutations, echoing back into Jira, or interfering with the teardown sweep — a TOCTOU race between harness assertions and live sync cycles.
findings.location: Scope & isolation section; AC line 'ZERO surviving test artifacts after a run'
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope & isolation section, add a prerequisite: 'The reconciler MUST NOT be running against the test project/label scope during harness execution. Either: (a) run the harness in a fully isolated local env with no reconciler daemon active, or (b) temporarily restrict the reconciler's JQL filter to exclude the test project/label and confirm the restriction is in place before the harness starts (and restored in the finally block).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether the new live-validation harness REPLACES the two existing bash probes in `scripts/jira-pressure-test/` or complements them, and document that decision in the plan — the existing probes already cover most of the 'comprehensive matrix' and the genuine gap is specifically the guaranteed bilateral teardown on failure, not the full matrix.
findings.finding: The plan proposes building a new live-validation harness whose 'comprehensive matrix' (create→bind, echo suppression, idempotency, blast-radius, status round-trip, cleanup) substantially overlaps two existing reference scripts — `scripts/jira-pressure-test/e2e_validation_probe.sh` (phases 1-6: create, outbound, inbound, idempotency, reconcile-check, cleanup) and `scripts/jira-pressure-test/e2e_field_validation_probe.sh` (10-ticket full-field CRUD matrix) — without stating whether the new harness extends, replaces, or is a third parallel live-test system alongside them.
findings.location: What section / Acceptance Criteria (AC-1, AC-2)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Relationship to existing probes' subsection: 'The new harness supersedes `e2e_validation_probe.sh` and `e2e_field_validation_probe.sh`; those scripts are retired after this epic' (if replacement) OR 'The new harness focuses solely on the guaranteed-teardown gap; the field-matrix scenarios re-use the existing probe infrastructure.' This avoids a silent third parallel system.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the default value of N (e.g., 'N = 3 consecutive passes with no status mutation') so the executor can implement the oscillation check without guessing.
findings.finding: 'N passes' in the oscillation test is undefined; the executor cannot know what value of N constitutes a sufficient proof of no-oscillation.
findings.location: What — matrix bullet: 'no oscillation over N passes'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'N passes' with 'three consecutive passes' (or whichever value the author intends).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the artifact-sweep as a concrete, anchored command (e.g., `jira issue list --jql 'project=TEST AND labels=rebar-test-artifact' | jq '.issues | length == 0'` for Jira; and a named local command for the local store) with an expected exit/result of zero artifacts.
findings.finding: The 'ZERO surviving test artifacts' AC lacks a concrete proving command: no JQL query, no local command, and no expected output are specified for the leaked-artifact sweep.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'ZERO surviving test artifacts after a run: test issues hard-deleted in Jira; test tickets archived/deleted locally; a leaked-artifact sweep runs before AND after'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Require the leaked-artifact sweep to emit a structured report (count and IDs of artifacts found and remediated) to the run log, so operators can detect recurring teardown failures.
findings.finding: The leaked-artifact sweep (pre- and post-run) has no specified instrumentation or output format — if the sweep finds and removes stale artifacts from a prior crashed run, this event is not surfaced to the operator, making silent partial-state cleanup invisible.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3 ('a leaked-artifact sweep runs before AND after')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the undefined shorthand codes 'C1 signal,' 'C2,' 'C3,' 'C4' in the What section with their full names (e.g., 'conflict signal,' 'hard-delete/re-create,' 'field-allowlist-drop alert,' '429/Retry-After') to avoid collision with the codebase's existing C1/C2 ref-lock terminology.
findings.finding: The plan uses 'C1 signal,' 'C2,' 'C3,' 'C4' as shorthand codes for reconciler behaviors (conflict signal, hard-delete/re-create, field-allowlist-drop alert, bulk-path 429/Retry-After) without defining them, while the codebase uses 'C1' and 'C2' unambiguously for ref-lock primitives in _advisory_lock.py and _ref_lock.py. This collision will cause implementers to look up the wrong concepts.
findings.location: What section: 'Hard-delete -> re-create (C2); field-allowlist-drop alert (C3); bulk-path 429/Retry-After (C4, fault-injected if needed)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify and cite the real implementation symbol or mechanism for 'echo suppression' (e.g., if it's the `_EXCLUDED_PREFIXES` tuple in `inbound_differ.py` or a write-pass marker), or explicitly note it as new behavior to be built as part of this harness.
findings.finding: The plan names 'echo-marked comments' and 'echo suppression' as a matrix scenario to validate, but no `echo_mark`, `ECHO_MARKER`, or equivalent symbol exists in the codebase. If echo suppression is implemented under a different name, the plan needs to cite the real symbol; if it is not yet implemented, exercising it is out-of-scope new behavior.
findings.location: What section — matrix bullet: 'Echo suppression: our own writes (echo-marked comments, status, fields) are NOT re-ingested inbound.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the grace-period duration or reference the config key that sets it (e.g., 'grace = 2 reconciler passes, controlled by tombstone_grace_passes config') so the harness can simulate and assert the correct window.
findings.finding: The 'grace' period in the deletion/tombstone scenario is undefined; an executor cannot implement the grace-period assertion without knowing its duration.
findings.location: What — matrix bullet: 'Jira 404 -> grace -> reversible retire'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit ACs for idempotency (e.g., 'Two consecutive reconciler passes on an unchanged remote produce mutation_count = 0, verified by asserting the reconciler's returned mutation count') and blast-radius breaker (e.g., 'Breaker does NOT trip on a normal pass; trips and emits a log/signal when synthetic mass-change exceeds threshold N').
findings.finding: The idempotency ('mutation_count = 0') and blast-radius breaker matrix items are not covered by any Acceptance Criterion; they are described in the What section but have no AC that maps to them, so the plan has no completion signal for these two behaviors.
findings.location: What — matrix bullets ('Idempotency: two consecutive passes…' and 'Blast-radius breaker…'); Acceptance Criteria section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Require harness configuration (Jira base URL, test project key, scoping JQL/label, local env root) to be externalized as environment variables or a config file — no hardcoded project keys or paths in harness code.
findings.finding: The harness configuration (which Jira project/URL, which local env path, test-scoping JQL/label) is not required to be externalized as configurable parameters — risking hardcoded project keys or env paths that break re-runnability across environments or require code edits to switch between test and prod targets.
findings.location: Scope & Isolation section and AC4 ('Isolation approach chosen and documented')
findings.suggested_fix: Define a `.env.test` or `harness_config.toml` with fields like `JIRA_TEST_PROJECT`, `JIRA_BASE_URL`, `TEST_SCOPE_LABEL`, `LOCAL_ENV_ROOT`; harness reads from these at startup and fails fast if unset.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define what 'field-allowlist-drop alert (C3)' means in concrete codebase terms: identify the specific alert type/event produced when a field is dropped from the sync allowlist, or remove C3 from the matrix if no such mechanism exists.
findings.finding: The plan references 'field-allowlist-drop alert (C3)' as a matrix item to validate, implying a codebase concept of a 'field allowlist' whose removal triggers an alert. No such `field_allowlist`, `FIELD_ALLOWLIST`, `allowed_fields`, or `field-allowlist` symbol exists anywhere in src/rebar (comprehensive grep returns zero matches). The C3 label is undefined in the plan body, making this an unverifiable assertion.
findings.location: What section — 'field-allowlist-drop alert (C3)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace opaque codes C1–C4 in the What matrix with the actual symbol/behavior names they correspond to (e.g., C1 → the conflict-resolution signal emitted by the conflict resolver; C3 → identify whether a 'field-allowlist-drop alert' feature exists or needs creating; C4 → cite `_call_with_retry` and clarify whether 'bulk-path' 429 is distinct from per-call).
findings.finding: The plan references 'C1 signal', 'C2', 'C3 (field-allowlist-drop alert)', and 'C4 (bulk-path 429/Retry-After)' as named test targets, but none of these labels correspond to any discoverable symbol, constant, or documented concept in the codebase. 'C3 field-allowlist-drop alert' in particular has no matching `field_allowlist`, `ALLOWED_FIELDS`, or alert implementation — making the matrix item unexecutable as written.
findings.location: What section — matrix bullet: 'Hard-delete -> re-create (C2); field-allowlist-drop alert (C3); bulk-path 429/Retry-After (C4, fault-injected if needed)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit preconditions section listing JIRA_URL, JIRA_USER, JIRA_API_TOKEN, JIRA_PROJECT, REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL=1, and `acli` binary presence as required before the harness runs, matching the pattern in tests/external/.
findings.finding: The plan never states the auth/environment preconditions the harness requires to run: JIRA_URL, JIRA_USER, JIRA_API_TOKEN (env-only secret), JIRA_PROJECT, REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL=1, and the `acli` binary on PATH. Without these documented, an executor cannot determine whether the environment is ready before attempting any live mutation.
findings.location: ## Scope & isolation / Acceptance Criteria
findings.suggested_fix: Add to 'Scope & isolation': 'Preconditions: JIRA_URL, JIRA_USER, JIRA_API_TOKEN (env-only), JIRA_PROJECT, REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL=1, and the `acli` binary on PATH must all be present; the harness must self-skip (not fail) if any are absent, matching tests/external/conftest.py gating.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document whether the test account has Jira project-creation permissions; if not, make label-scoped isolation in a designated throwaway project the primary (not fallback) approach, and name the specific project and label convention.
findings.finding: The plan prefers 'a dedicated Jira test project' for isolation but does not verify that project-creation permissions are available. Jira Cloud project creation typically requires site-admin or project-admin privileges that a standard API token may not carry; if unavailable, the harness silently falls back to the shared project, risking production pollution.
findings.location: ## Scope & isolation

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify exactly which counter(s) constitute the idempotency signal (e.g., 'reconcile_once's returned mutation_count AND local_mutation_count_at_pass must both equal 0 on the second consecutive pass') to remove ambiguity for an executing agent.
findings.finding: The idempotency test criterion 'two consecutive passes on an unchanged remote -> mutation_count = 0' references an ambiguous counter. The codebase exposes at least three distinct counts: `mutation_count` (pass-level), `local_mutation_count` (health), and `unfiltered_mutation_count` (pre-blast-radius filter). An agent implementing the idempotency check cannot determine which count must be zero.
findings.location: What section — 'Idempotency: two consecutive passes on an unchanged remote -> mutation_count = 0'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'mutation_count = 0' with 'reconcile_once returns {"mutation_count": 0} AND the health record shows local_mutation_count_at_pass=0 on the second consecutive call with an unchanged Jira snapshot.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
